1	[Task task-discussion-thread-002] PHASE 3a: PLAN
2	Title: Test DiscussionThread component
3	
4	Read .fleet/tasks/task-discussion-thread-002/questions.json and .fleet/tasks/task-discussion-thread-002/research.json
5	for context on what was asked and answered.
6	
7	YOU MUST create .fleet/tasks/task-discussion-thread-002/plan.json with this structure:
8	{
9	  "phase": "plan",
10	  "decisions": [
11	    { "id": "d1", "decision": "...", "based_on": ["q1"], "alternatives_rejected": ["..."], "reversibility": "easy|medium|hard" }
12	  ],
13	  "steps": [
14	    { "step": 1, "task": "...", "depends_on": [] }
15	  ],
16	  "spawn_children": false,
17	  "children": []
18	}
19	
20	DO NOT STOP until plan.json exists and contains at least one step.
21	Verify: ls -la .fleet/tasks/task-discussion-thread-002/ to confirm the file was written.
22	
23	DECOMPOSITION: Set spawn_children=true for independent sub-tasks that warrant
24	their own 4-phase workflow. Format: { "id": "...", "title": "...", "description": "..." }
25	
26	BEFORE YOU STOP: git add -A && git commit --no-verify -m "plan: task-discussion-thread-002"

1	{
2	  "phase": "question",
3	  "questions": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "q1",
6	      "question": "Should formatRelativeTime be mocked or should we use fixed timestamps far enough in the past to produce deterministic output (e.g., '1d ago')? The Analytics test doesn't face this issue since it doesn't render relative times.",
7	      "category": "design",
8	      "why_it_matters": "formatRelativeTime depends on Date.now(), so without mocking time or the function, timestamp assertions will be flaky across test runs."
9	    },
10	    {
11	      "id": "q2",
12	      "question": "The task says 'Use mocks for useMarketComments, usePostMarketComment, useSession' — the codebase pattern (TradingPanel, EventTradePanel, etc.) mocks @/features/session/context with vi.importActual spread and @/lib/query/hooks with vi.fn() stubs. Should we follow this exact pattern or mock the API client layer like Analytics.test.tsx does?",
13	      "category": "codebase",
14	      "why_it_matters": "The component uses hooks directly (not the API client), so mocking hooks is the right approach and matches the task description, but confirming alignment with the established pattern ensures consistency."
15	    },
16	    {
17	      "id": "q3",
18	      "question": "For test 10 (submit posts comment and clears form on success), the component calls crypto.randomUUID() for the idempotency key. Is crypto.randomUUID available in the vitest/jsdom environment, or does it need to be stubbed?",
19	      "category": "codebase",
20	      "why_it_matters": "If jsdom doesn't provide crypto.randomUUID, the submit handler will throw, breaking the test. We may need to mock it globally or via vi.stubGlobal."
21	    },
22	    {
23	      "id": "q4",
24	      "question": "The mutation mock needs to simulate the onSuccess callback to verify form clearing. The existing pattern (TradingPanel) uses mockMutate.mockImplementation to capture and invoke the onSuccess callback. Should we follow the same pattern where mutate is mocked to synchronously call options.onSuccess?",
25	      "category": "design",
26	      "why_it_matters": "The form-clear assertion (test 10) depends on the onSuccess callback firing. Getting the mock implementation right is critical for this test to work."
27	    }
28	  ],
29	  "assumptions": [
30	    {
31	      "id": "a1",
32	      "assumption": "The mocking pattern should follow TradingPanel.test.tsx / EventTradePanel.test.tsx style: mock @/features/session/context with vi.importActual spread + useSession: vi.fn(), and mock @/lib/query/hooks with the specific hooks as vi.fn().",
33	      "confidence": 0.95,
34	      "if_wrong": "Would need to restructure the entire mock setup — either mocking the API client layer or using a different hook mocking strategy."
35	    },
36	    {
37	      "id": "a2",
38	      "assumption": "The useMarketComments hook mock should return an object matching react-query's UseQueryResult shape: { isLoading, isError, error, data } with data being MarketCommentsResponse | undefined.",
39	      "confidence": 0.95,
40	      "if_wrong": "The component accesses comments.data.comments and comments.data.pagination.returned — if the mock shape is wrong, tests will throw unexpected errors."
41	    },
42	    {
43	      "id": "a3",
44	      "assumption": "The usePostMarketComment hook mock should return an object matching react-query's UseMutationResult shape: { mutate, isPending, isError, error } with mutate being a vi.fn().",
45	      "confidence": 0.95,
46	      "if_wrong": "The component checks mutation.isPending and mutation.isError — wrong shape would cause runtime errors in tests."
47	    },
48	    {
49	      "id": "a4",
50	      "assumption": "renderWithProviders from helpers.tsx can be used directly since it wraps with SessionProvider, but since we mock useSession at the module level, the real SessionProvider is still rendered (it just won't be used by the component).",
51	      "confidence": 0.9,
52	      "if_wrong": "If SessionProvider interferes with the mocked useSession, we may need a simpler render wrapper or skip the session provider."
53	    },
54	    {
55	      "id": "a5",
56	      "assumption": "crypto.randomUUID() is available in vitest's environment (either via Node's crypto or jsdom polyfill), or can be trivially stubbed with vi.stubGlobal.",
57	      "confidence": 0.8,
58	      "if_wrong": "Would need to add a global mock for crypto.randomUUID in the test setup."
59	    },
60	    {
61	      "id": "a6",
62	      "assumption": "For the read-only test, any non-'active' status (e.g., 'resolved') should trigger the read-only message. The component checks market.status !== 'active'.",
63	      "confidence": 1.0,
64	      "if_wrong": "N/A — this is directly visible in the component source code."
65	    },
66	    {
67	      "id": "a7",
68	      "assumption": "formatRelativeTime can be handled by either mocking vi.spyOn or by using a fixed date via vi.useFakeTimers, rather than mocking the utility module.",
69	      "confidence": 0.85,
70	      "if_wrong": "Would need to mock @/lib/utils/format module to control the timestamp output."
71	    },
72	    {
73	      "id": "a8",
74	      "assumption": "The comment count badge uses pagination.returned (not comments.length), so the mock data must have consistent pagination.returned values.",
75	      "confidence": 1.0,
76	      "if_wrong": "N/A — directly visible in component source: comments.data.pagination.returned."
77	    }
78	  ],
79	  "spawn_children": false,
80	  "children": []
81	}
82

1	{
2	  "phase": "research",
3	  "answers": [
4	    {
5	      "question_id": "q1",
6	      "answer": "Use vi.useFakeTimers() with a fixed system time (e.g., vi.setSystemTime('2025-01-15T12:00:00Z')). Then create comment timestamps relative to that fixed time (e.g., '2025-01-14T12:00:00Z' → '1d ago'). formatRelativeTime uses Date.now() internally (format.ts:17), so faking timers makes its output fully deterministic without mocking the module. This is cleaner than module-level mocking and more composable. The TradingPanel/EventTradePanel tests don't face this because they don't render relative timestamps.",
7	      "confidence": 0.95,
8	      "evidence": [
9	        "frontend/src/lib/utils/format.ts:17 — const diff = Date.now() - new Date(iso).getTime();",
10	        "frontend/src/lib/utils/format.ts:18-22 — time bucket logic (mins, hours, days)",
11	        "frontend/src/features/market/DiscussionThread.tsx:77 — formatRelativeTime(comment.createdAt)"
12	      ],
13	      "uncertainty": "Minor: need to call vi.useRealTimers() in afterEach to avoid leaking fake timers into other tests. Also vi.useFakeTimers may affect setTimeout/setInterval if react-query uses them internally, but this is standard practice."
14	    },
15	    {
16	      "question_id": "q2",
17	      "answer": "Follow the TradingPanel/EventTradePanel hook-mocking pattern exactly. Mock @/features/session/context with async vi.importActual spread + useSession: vi.fn(), and mock @/lib/query/hooks with useMarketComments: vi.fn() and usePostMarketComment: vi.fn(). This matches the task description and the established codebase pattern. The Analytics.test.tsx API-client mocking pattern is not appropriate here because DiscussionThread consumes hooks directly (not the API client), and all other component tests that use hooks follow the hook-mock pattern.",
18	      "confidence": 0.98,
19	      "evidence": [
20	        "frontend/src/__tests__/TradingPanel.test.tsx:13-21 — vi.mock('@/features/session/context', async () => { const actual = await vi.importActual(...); return { ...actual, useSession: vi.fn() }; })",
21	        "frontend/src/__tests__/TradingPanel.test.tsx:23-25 — vi.mock('@/lib/query/hooks', () => ({ useProbabilityEdit: vi.fn() }))",
22	        "frontend/src/__tests__/EventTradePanel.test.tsx:13-25 — identical pattern",
23	        "frontend/src/features/market/DiscussionThread.tsx:2-3 — imports useSession, useMarketComments, usePostMarketComment"
24	      ],
25	      "uncertainty": "None — two established precedents in the codebase confirm this pattern."
26	    },
27	    {
28	      "question_id": "q3",
29	      "answer": "crypto.randomUUID() IS available in the vitest/jsdom environment. Vitest runs on Node.js, which provides crypto.randomUUID globally (available since Node 19+, and widely available in modern Node). Confirmed by running `node -e \"console.log(typeof crypto.randomUUID === 'function')\"` → true. The existing TradingPanel.test.tsx exercises the submit path (fireEvent.click at line 152) which calls crypto.randomUUID() in the component handler (TradingPanel.tsx) without any stub or polyfill, and the test passes. No stubbing needed.",
30	      "confidence": 0.95,
31	      "evidence": [
32	        "frontend/src/__tests__/TradingPanel.test.tsx:152 — fireEvent.click(button) triggers handler that calls crypto.randomUUID()",
33	        "frontend/src/__tests__/TradingPanel.test.tsx:163 — expect(call.payload.idempotencyKey).toBeDefined() confirms it works",
34	        "frontend/src/features/market/DiscussionThread.tsx:34 — crypto.randomUUID() usage",
35	        "Node.js runtime check: crypto.randomUUID is a function → true"
36	      ],
37	      "uncertainty": "Very minor: if CI uses an extremely old Node version (<15.x), crypto.randomUUID might not be available, but this is unlikely given the project's modern dependencies."
38	    },
39	    {
40	      "question_id": "q4",
41	      "answer": "Yes, use mockMutate.mockImplementation to synchronously invoke the onSuccess callback. The DiscussionThread component passes onSuccess as the second argument to mutation.mutate (line 38-42): mutation.mutate({ payload, session }, { onSuccess: () => setBody('') }). The mock should capture and invoke this: mockMutate.mockImplementation((_variables, options) => { options?.onSuccess?.(); }). This is NOT the same pattern as TradingPanel (which doesn't test onSuccess at all — it only verifies the payload). This is a custom extension of the mockMutate pattern specifically for DiscussionThread's form-clear behavior. The implementation should be set in the specific test (test 10), not globally, so other tests keep the default vi.fn() behavior.",
42	      "confidence": 0.92,
43	      "evidence": [
44	        "frontend/src/features/market/DiscussionThread.tsx:29-43 — mutation.mutate call with { onSuccess: () => setBody('') } as second arg",
45	        "frontend/src/__tests__/TradingPanel.test.tsx:139-165 — submit test only checks payload, no onSuccess testing",
46	        "frontend/src/__tests__/TradingPanel.test.tsx:11 — const mockMutate = vi.fn() pattern"
47	      ],
48	      "uncertainty": "The TanStack Query mutate() signature is mutate(variables, options?) where options includes onSuccess. The mock needs to match this signature. We should verify that the onSuccess callback in the options argument (second arg to mutate) is the one the component provides, not the hook-level onSuccess."
49	    }
50	  ],
51	  "assumption_checks": [
52	    {
53	      "assumption_id": "a1",
54	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
55	      "confidence": 0.98,
56	      "impact": "Proceed with TradingPanel/EventTradePanel mock pattern. Mock @/features/session/context with vi.importActual + useSession: vi.fn(), and @/lib/query/hooks with useMarketComments: vi.fn() + usePostMarketComment: vi.fn()."
57	    },
58	    {
59	      "assumption_id": "a2",
60	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
61	      "confidence": 0.95,
62	      "impact": "Mock useMarketComments to return UseQueryResult-shaped object. Component accesses .data.comments, .data.pagination.returned, .isLoading, .isError, .error. A defaultQueryState helper (similar to defaultMutationState) would be useful."
63	    },
64	    {
65	      "assumption_id": "a3",
66	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
67	      "confidence": 0.95,
68	      "impact": "Mock usePostMarketComment to return UseMutationResult-shaped object with mockMutate. Component accesses .mutate, .isPending, .isError, .error. Reuse the defaultMutationState pattern from TradingPanel."
69	    },
70	    {
71	      "assumption_id": "a4",
72	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
73	      "confidence": 0.95,
74	      "impact": "renderWithProviders wraps with SessionProvider (helpers.tsx:22-29), but since useSession is mocked at the module level with vi.mock(), the real SessionProvider context is never read by the component. Safe to use renderWithProviders as-is."
75	    },
76	    {
77	      "assumption_id": "a5",
78	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
79	      "confidence": 0.95,
80	      "impact": "No crypto.randomUUID stub needed. Node.js provides it globally and TradingPanel tests already exercise it successfully."
81	    },
82	    {
83	      "assumption_id": "a6",
84	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
85	      "confidence": 1.0,
86	      "impact": "Use market.status = 'resolved' (or any non-'active' value) to trigger read-only mode. Component checks market.status !== 'active' at line 21."
87	    },
88	    {
89	      "assumption_id": "a7",
90	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
91	      "confidence": 0.9,
92	      "impact": "vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.setSystemTime() is the preferred approach. This controls Date.now() which formatRelativeTime depends on, producing deterministic output without module-level mocking. Remember to restore with vi.useRealTimers() in afterEach."
93	    },
94	    {
95	      "assumption_id": "a8",
96	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
97	      "confidence": 1.0,
98	      "impact": "Mock data must have consistent pagination.returned values. Component renders '{pagination.returned} comment(s)' at line 55. Also handles singular/plural: 'comment' vs 'comments' based on === 1 check."
99	    }
100	  ],
101	  "spawn_children": false,
102	  "children": []
103	}
104

1. Setting up mocks (useSession, useMarketComments, usePostMarketComment)
2. Creating fixtures (market, session, comment data)
3. Writing tests for all the component states

content.text: Plan created and committed. The plan has 4 decisions (fake timers for deterministic timestamps, hook-level mocking pattern, no crypto stub needed, per-test mockImplementation for onSuccess) and 17 steps covering the full test file: mock setup, fixtures, beforeEach/afterEach, 13 individual tests (loading, error, empty, comments rendering, count badge, unconfigured session, composer form, read-only, disabled button, submit+clear, pending state, mutation error, character count), and a final verification run.

result: Plan created and committed. The plan has 4 decisions (fake timers for deterministic timestamps, hook-level mocking pattern, no crypto stub needed, per-test mockImplementation for onSuccess) and 17 steps covering the full test file: mock setup, fixtures, beforeEach/afterEach, 13 individual tests (loading, error, empty, comments rendering, count badge, unconfigured session, composer form, read-only, disabled button, submit+clear, pending state, mutation error, character count), and a final verification run.
